places.name: The Neon Museum
places.description: A program of Communist glamour in post-Stalin Poland is preserved in this colorful museum.

places.name: Galeria Forty / Forty
places.description: A hidden art gallery in an abandoned Warsaw fort.

places.name: Chopin's Heart
places.description: After the famous composer died in Paris, his sister snuck his heart out of the country to honor his wish that it be buried in their native country.

places.name: Czar PRL: Life Under Communism Museum
places.description: This hidden collection of everyday objects illustrates life within communist Poland.

places.name: Keret House
places.description: The world’s narrowest house makes for an awkward, four-foot-wide living space.

places.name: Warsaw Uprising Monument
places.description: A tribute to the Polish insurgents who fought in the failed attempt to end their city's Nazi occupation.

places.name: Oberża Pod Czerwonym Wieprzem
places.description: Order “Proletariat’s Cutlet” at this communist-themed restaurant allegedly once visited by Lenin.
